content.observe: The closer memory sits to the CPU, the faster it answers and the less of it there is. That's not a design flaw — it's the whole strategy.
content.fault: You asked for a value the cache didn't have. That's a miss — and now you're paying the price of a much slower level.

hotspots.label: Registers
hotspots.detail: Fastest possible access, inside the CPU itself. Tiny capacity — a handful of values.

hotspots.label: Cache (L1/L2/L3)
hotspots.detail: Small, fast memory that keeps recently-used data close to the CPU.

hotspots.label: Main Memory (RAM)
hotspots.detail: Larger and slower than cache, but still far faster than disk.

hotspots.label: Disk / Secondary Storage
hotspots.detail: Huge capacity, huge latency compared to everything above it.
